name: Org Structure
description: Map reporting lines, roles, and team boundaries across an organization
systemPromptHint: Each node should represent a role or team, not a person — people change, structures persist. Reporting lines must be unambiguous: a node with multiple managers signals a matrix structure that should be made explicit. Span of control matters — more than 7-8 direct reports usually indicates a missing layer. If the diagram is flat everywhere, critical coordination points are being hidden.

nodeTags.name: Department
nodeTags.description: A major organizational division or business unit

nodeTags.name: Team
nodeTags.description: A working group within a department

nodeTags.name: Vacant
nodeTags.description: An unfilled position or planned role

derivedIndicators.name: Top Leadership
derivedIndicators.description: No one above — the top of the reporting chain

derivedIndicators.name: Manager
derivedIndicators.description: Has both a reporting line above and direct reports below

derivedIndicators.name: Individual Contributor
derivedIndicators.description: No direct reports — an end-of-chain role
